description: Toyota is expanding its San Antonio manufacturing campus with a new $2 billion vehicle assembly facility (Project Orca) that will create 2,000 jobs. The facility is expected to begin construction in 2026 and become operational in 2030.

United Foods International established a new 126,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Phoenix capable of processing 8 million gallons of liquid food products annually. The facility handles formulation, liquid processing, packaging, and distribution of food ingredient products for commercial customers.

Clarios is expanding and modernizing its existing automotive battery manufacturing facility and distribution center in St. Joseph, Missouri to increase production capacity. The company will receive $5.6 million in tax credits through the Missouri Works Program tied to job creation and investment requirements.

Southern Classic Food Group is expanding its existing Brundidge, Alabama production facility with new manufacturing lines, equipment upgrades, and supporting infrastructure to support growing demand and enhance operational efficiency.

GHH-BONATRANS is constructing a new manufacturing facility at Wayland Business Park in New York to produce railway wheelsets, axles, and related rail components. Production is scheduled to begin in late 2028 and will serve customers across the U.S. and Canada.

Niagara Bottling is constructing a 362,000-square-foot manufacturing and warehousing facility at Tryon Technology Park in Perth, New York, specializing in private label bottling, packaging, and production for beverage brands. Operations are scheduled to begin in early 2027.
Hyundai Motor Group's first steelmaking venture in North America, an electric arc furnace mill in Louisiana focused on the automotive sector. Posco invested $582 million as a 20% stakeholder.

USG Corp. will invest $1.18 billion to build a new drywall and gypsum-based construction materials production facility in Orange, Texas, with an initial project investment of $650 million and expected creation of nearly 200 jobs. Construction was previously set to begin fall 2025 and reach operational status by early 2028.

U.S. Steel (owned by Nippon Steel) announced a $1.9 billion investment in a direct reduced iron plant at Big River Works in Osceola, Arkansas, with completion expected in Q1 2029. This is part of Nippon Steel's $11+ billion commitment to U.S. Steel investments following its acquisition.

Kalmbach Feeds is constructing a new manufacturing facility for K9 & Kin Brands in Wyandot County, Ohio to produce premium dog and cat food products. The $125 million investment, the largest private expansion in the county's history, is expected to begin production in 2027.

Reinhausen is expanding its existing manufacturing facility in Humboldt, Tennessee to increase production of advanced transformer control systems, voltage regulation systems, and grid-automation technologies for power transmission and distribution networks.

Lockers Manufacturing is expanding its Batesville, Mississippi production operations through the purchase of an existing facility and addition of new production capabilities for manufacturing custom storage and locker systems for commercial, institutional, and industrial applications.

Acra Cast, Inc. is expanding its existing foundry operations in Bay City, Michigan with additional production space, new equipment purchases, and workforce growth to support increased demand for American-made metal casting components for industrial, commercial, defense, and automotive applications.

LEV Manufacturing will establish a 100,000-square-foot assembly, logistics, and fulfillment center in Putnam County, Tennessee to serve as its primary U.S. hub for electric mobility brands including Rad Power Bikes, Serial 1, and Life EV. The facility will support design, assembly, and distribution of light electric vehicles and related components.

Stella-Jones Corporation is constructing a new steel lattice tower manufacturing facility in Fayetteville, Tennessee. This is the company's first U.S.-based steel structure manufacturing facility, with production scheduled to begin by the end of 2027.

Adrian Steel Company is expanding its Lenawee County, Michigan facility by 112,000 square feet to enhance capabilities in raw material storage, cutting, forming, welding, painting, assembly, and shipping. The company designs and manufactures shelving, partitions, racks, and accessories for work vans and trucks.

Marubeni-Itochu Steel America plans to construct an advanced flat-rolled steel processing facility in Osceola, Arkansas, adjacent to Big River Steel, to produce steel for automotive and other industries across the South-Central U.S.

Ferrara Candy Company is constructing a 750,000-square-foot manufacturing facility in Orangeburg County, South Carolina to produce and package confectionery products including gummies, chocolates, and sugar-based candies. Production is scheduled to begin in Q1 2029.

Virginia Transformer Corporation is expanding its Rincon, Georgia production facility with advanced manufacturing equipment, upgraded material handling systems, and an automated warehouse to increase production capacity by 50 percent. The facility manufactures power transformers and electrical equipment for utility, industrial, and infrastructure applications.

AbbVie is constructing a new pharmaceutical production campus on 185 acres in Durham, North Carolina, integrating advanced manufacturing and laboratory technologies with AI to support immunology, neuroscience, and oncology medicines. The project represents AbbVie's largest capital investment to date and is expected to create over 700 jobs.

Joint venture between Sandvik and Alpha Metallurgical Resources establishing a new 100,000-square-foot manufacturing facility at Waterway Industrial Park to produce rock bolts and resin capsules for mining and infrastructure ground support applications, representing Sandvik's re-entry into the U.S. ground support market.

Gowan Milling is expanding its existing grain milling and processing facility in Blytheville, Arkansas to increase production capabilities for food and feed applications. The expansion supports growing customer demand and strengthens the company's North American operations.

Gorton's, a Nissui Group subsidiary, opened a new seafood processing factory in Lebanon, Indiana to increase North American production capacity and produce value-added products, complementing its primary facility in Gloucester, Massachusetts.

Givaudan is constructing a new state-of-the-art liquids production facility in Reading, Ohio, representing an initial investment of $215 million USD. The 24,000 square metre facility will create over 300 jobs and is expected to begin partial operations in 2027.

Belgian industrial storage and automation company Stow Group will build a $36 million manufacturing facility in Adairsville, Georgia to produce industrial racks and warehousing products, creating approximately 200 jobs to serve food and beverage, logistics, and life sciences sectors.

American Packaging Corporation opened a new 215,000 square-foot flexible packaging manufacturing facility in Chili, NY, enabling the company to hire 70 new employees at the site and plan up to 260 new full-time jobs over five years. The State of New York provided $14.7 million in performance-based incentives to secure the expansion.

Apple will build a new 250,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility in Houston to produce servers for Apple Intelligence and Private Cloud Compute. The facility is expected to open in 2026 and create thousands of jobs, with server production beginning later in 2025.

Ziehl-Abegg will construct a new 46,500 square meter manufacturing plant in Winston-Salem, North Carolina, representing the company's largest single investment in its 112-year history. The facility will produce energy-efficient fans and HVAC equipment with high vertical integration including engine manufacturing, welding, and CNC operations, with production transitioning from the existing Greensboro site in 2025.

American Battery Factory is constructing a $1.2 billion advanced battery gigafactory on Tucson's south side at the Aerospace Research Campus, with plans to produce lithium-iron phosphate battery cells using Honeywell's AI-enabled automated manufacturing equipment. The facility is targeted for completion by January 2025 and is expected to employ approximately 1,000 workers at full buildout.

Hitachi's new railway rolling stock factory in Hagerstown, Maryland has commenced full operations, supplying railcars for North American operators including Washington D.C. Metro. The $100 million facility produces up to 20 cars per month and relocated operations from Florida, creating approximately 1,300 jobs.

Base Power plans to invest $265 million in a new 486,000-square-foot battery manufacturing facility near Austin-Bergstrom International Airport, creating 500 new full-time jobs. The facility will manufacture residential batteries for the company's growing energy storage business.

Cambrex is expanding its API manufacturing capacity in Charles City, Iowa with a new large-scale facility featuring 140,000 liters of additional capacity for complex chemistries including highly potent active pharmaceutical ingredients and peptide manufacturing. The $120 million US investment includes advanced equipment and reactor systems with groundbreaking expected in late 2026.

Hankook Tire completed expansion of its Tennessee plant to double passenger car and light truck tire production capacity from 5.5 million to 11 million units annually, and add 1 million truck and bus tire capacity. The company is conducting trial production with plans to reach full operational capacity by end of 2026, aiming to increase local North American production from 25% to approximately 50% of sales to reduce tariffs and logistics costs.

Stellantis announced a $13 billion U.S. manufacturing expansion to reopen key plants, launch five new vehicle models, and create more than 5,000 jobs across multiple states by 2029. The investment reflects the company's strategy to expand North American capacity and address tariff headwinds, with production shifting from Canada to the U.S.

LG Energy Solution is expanding its existing EV battery manufacturing facility in Holland, Michigan with a $1.7 billion investment that will increase battery production capacity fivefold and create 1,200 jobs. The expansion will manufacture advanced long cell design batteries with improved energy density for electric vehicles.

Clarios announced a $6 billion expansion plan for U.S. battery manufacturing through 2035, including $2.5 billion for advanced battery production (AGM and energy storage), $1.9 billion for critical minerals processing and recovery, $1 billion for next-generation technologies (sodium-ion, AI storage), and $600 million for facility modernization.
Ferrero completed a $75 million expansion of its Bloomington, Illinois chocolate factory, adding 70,000 square feet to produce chocolate for Kinder, Ferrero Rocher, Butterfinger, and CRUNCH products.

CSL announced a $1.5 billion expansion of US manufacturing operations over five years to increase production capacity for plasma-derived therapies including immunoglobulin treatments. The investment will create hundreds of high-quality jobs across scientific and advanced manufacturing roles while strengthening U.S. therapeutic self-sufficiency.

Ferrero is investing $214.4 million to expand its Bloomington, Illinois manufacturing facility with a 169,000-square-foot expansion dedicated to producing Kinder Bueno premium chocolate bars, creating up to 200 new jobs over four years. This is Ferrero's first time manufacturing Kinder products in North America.

Eli Lilly is constructing a new $3.5 billion pharmaceutical manufacturing facility in Fogelsville, Pennsylvania (Lehigh Valley region) focused on injectables and device manufacturing, with emphasis on next-generation weight-loss therapies including retatrutide. The plant is expected to employ 850 people and come online in 2031.
Eli Lilly announced a $27 billion investment in U.S. manufacturing expansion across multiple domestic sites for pharmaceutical manufacturing.

Siemens Energy is investing $1 billion across multiple US manufacturing facilities to expand grid component production. Primary investment includes a new high-voltage switchgear facility in Mississippi with expansions in North Carolina (power transformers, gas turbines), Florida (gas turbine blades), Alabama (electrical components), and other states, creating over 1,500 new jobs.

American Spraytech is expanding its personal care spray manufacturing operations by acquiring the 18,600-square-foot Hahn's Woodworking facility on Meister Avenue in Branchburg, NJ to add a high-speed production line with propellant storage tanks. The expansion is part of the company's growth strategy to increase capacity from 72 million cans produced last year to an expected 100 million cans this year, serving major consumer companies including Unilever, Proctor & Gamble, and L'Oreal.

Eli Lilly is constructing four new pharmaceutical manufacturing sites in the United States, including locations in Research Triangle Park and Concord, North Carolina, Lebanon Indiana (home to the Lilly Medicine Foundry), and expanding existing facilities in Indianapolis and Kenosha County, Wisconsin. The expansion focuses on manufacturing active pharmaceutical ingredients, small molecule chemical synthesis, and parenteral manufacturing, with over $50 billion in total U.S. capital commitments since 2020.
